# [RISC-V] Miscompile at -O[23]

**Author:** ewlu
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/149335
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** backend:RISC-V, regression, miscompilation, release:backport, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2025-07-22T07:21:44Z

## Body

Testcase:
```c
unsigned short a[10];
void b(short c, short i[]) {
  for (short k = 1; k < 9; k += 2)
    a[k] = c ? i[k] : i[k + 1];
}
long long m;
short c;
short i[10];
void checksum() {
  for (int k = 0; k < 10; ++k)
    m ^= a[k] + (m < 6) + (m > 2);
}
int main() {
  for (int k = 0; k < 10; ++k)
    a[k] = 3;
  b(c, i);
  checksum();
  __builtin_printf("%llu\n", m);
}
```

Commands
```
# -O3
$ /scratch/ewlu/daily-upstream-build/build-gcv/bin/clang -march=rv64gcv -O3 red.c -o user-config.out -fsigned-char -fno-strict-aliasing -fwrapv 
$ QEMU_CPU=rv64,vlen=128,rvv_ta_all_1s=true,rvv_ma_all_1s=true,v=true,vext_spec=v1.0,zve32f=true,zve64f=true timeout --verbose -k 0.1 4 /scratch/ewlu/daily-upstream-build/build-gcv/bin/qemu-riscv64 user-config.out 1
65541

# -O2
$ /scratch/ewlu/daily-upstream-build/build-gcv/bin/clang -march=rv64gcv -O2 red.c -o user-config.out -fsigned-char -fno-strict-aliasing -fwrapv 
$ QEMU_CPU=rv64,vlen=128,rvv_ta_all_1s=true,rvv_ma_all_1s=true,v=true,vext_spec=v1.0,zve32f=true,zve64f=true timeout --verbose -k 0.1 4 /scratch/ewlu/daily-upstream-build/build-gcv/bin/qemu-riscv64 user-config.out 1
65541

# -O1
$ /scratch/ewlu/daily-upstream-build/build-gcv/bin/clang -march=rv64gcv -O1 red.c -o user-config.out -fsigned-char -fno-strict-aliasing -fwrapv 
$ QEMU_CPU=rv64,vlen=128,rvv_ta_all_1s=true,rvv_ma_all_1s=true,v=true,vext_spec=v1.0,zve32f=true,zve64f=true timeout --verbose -k 0.1 4 /scratch/ewlu/daily-upstream-build/build-gcv/bin/qemu-riscv64 user-config.out 1
0
```

Bisected to 60ad6e3fa45c03dc1fc7521ead7583a9a7d9fb61 as the first bad commit.

Found via fuzzer

## Comments

### Comment 1 - topperc

opt-bisect points to riscv-vector-peephole

CC @lukel97 

---

### Comment 2 - topperc

Good code

```
          li      a1, 85                                                           
          vsetivli        zero, 7, e16, m1, ta, ma                                 
          vmv.s.x v0, a1                                                           
          vle16.v v8, (a0), v0.t                                                   
          vsetivli        zero, 8, e16, m1, ta, ma                                 
          vmv.v.i v9, 3                                                            
          vmerge.vvm      v8, v9, v8, v0                                           
          addi    a0, s0, 18                                                       
          vse16.v v8, (a0) 
```

Bad code

```
        li      a1, 85                                                           
        vsetivli        zero, 8, e16, m1, ta, ma                                 
        vmv.s.x v0, a1                                                           
        vmv.v.i v8, 3                                                            
        vsetivli        zero, 7, e16, m1, ta, mu                                 
        vle16.v v8, (a0), v0.t                                                   
        addi    a0, s0, 18                                                       
        vsetivli        zero, 8, e16, m1, ta, ma                                 
        vse16.v v8, (a0) 
```

Looks like we changed the VL of the vmerge by combining it into the load.

---

### Comment 3 - topperc

I think the issue is that convertSameMaskVMergeToVMv turned a vmerge into vmv without noticing that the load had a different VL than the vmerge.

---
